title: [Code Quality] Add *.bak pattern to .gitignore to prevent future accidental commits
body:
### Description

Issue #537 identified and removes two stale `.bak` files (`tests/test_controld_profile.sh.bak`, `tests/test_dns_utils.sh.bak`) that were committed from a previous editing session. However, there is currently no `.gitignore` rule preventing `.bak` files from being tracked in future editing sessions.

Without this guard, any editor that creates backup files on save (vim's `swp`/`bak`, JetBrains `*.bak`, Sublime Text `.bak`) could silently introduce stale artifacts into the repo — which is exactly how the current ones ended up committed.

### Suggested Changes

Add a `*.bak` exclusion to `.gitignore`:

```gitignore
# Editor backup files
*.bak
```

If `.gitignore` already has an editor-artifacts section, add it there. Otherwise append it with a comment.

### Files Affected

- `.gitignore` — add one line

### Success Criteria

- [ ] `.gitignore` contains a `*.bak` pattern (or equivalent glob covering `.bak` files)
- [ ] `git check-ignore tests/test_controld_profile.sh.bak` returns the file as ignored
- [ ] No regressions in `make test` or `make lint`

### Source

Discovered while analyzing [Daily QAReport - March 4, 2026 (discussion #520)](https://github.com/abhimehro/personal-config/discussions/520) and the complementary cleanup issue #537. The `.bak` files were caught by the task miner but the root-cause prevention (ignoring the pattern) was not captured.

### Priority

**Low** — ~5-minute fix. Prevents a class of repo-hygiene issues from recurring after #537 removes the existing artifacts. Best done in the same PR as #537.
Related to #437
